title: Least privilege
description: Amaka slides a ticket across the desk: Tunde spotted /home/intern/config/secrets.env with world-writable perms on the training host. That is exactly the kind of misconfiguration The Griot loves. First confirm the problem with `stat /home/intern/config/secrets.env`, then tighten it with `chmod 600 /home/intern/config/secrets.env`. Once the file is restricted to owner-only access, `cat` it to read the confirmation line Amaka stamped inside. That line is your flag.

welcome: Run `stat` on it, then `chmod 600` to bring it back to least privilege.
hints: Start with `stat /home/intern/config/secrets.env` to see the current mode.
World-writable (mode 666) means owner, group, and world can all write. Fix it with `chmod 600 /home/intern/config/secrets.env`.
After locking it down, `cat /home/intern/config/secrets.env` to read Amaka's confirmation line.

files./home/intern/README.md:
Amaka: routine hardening drill. Every sensitive file on this host must be 600 (owner rw, nothing else).
If you ever see 666 on something with the word `secret` in it, fix it before you touch anything else.
files./home/intern/config/README:
Config directory. Contains environment files consumed by the Sankofa training services.
Policy: *.env files must be mode 600. Anything looser is an audit finding.
